name: Biscotti Di Consuolo (Consolation Biscuits)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Mild olive oil, or melted butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Although these biscuits have an unhappy association, they are tender and tasty. The name refers to the time during which a bereaved family receives callers and serves them these biscotti with a glass of wine.
2. In Campania and the Basilicata, these customs survive in out-of-the-way mountain villages that still cling to ancient traditions.
3. Combine the flour, salt, and baking powder in a mixing bowl.
4. Combine the egg, honey, and olive oil or butter in a bowl, beat to mix, and pour into the flour mixture.
5. Stir well to form a firm dough.
6. Remove the dough from the bowl to a floured surface and knead well, about 2 or 3 minutes.
7. Flour the dough lightly, wrap in plastic, and allow to rest at room temperature about 1 hour.
8. Unwrap the dough, cut it into 12 pieces, and roll each piece into a rope about 1/4 inch in diameter x 12 inches long.
9. Divide each rope into three pieces. Join the ends and form each piece into a figure eight, making sure that the seam lies at the midpoint of the figure eight and underneath it.
10. Transfer the biscotti to parchment lined cookie sheets.
11. Bake them in a preheated 350 degree F oven for about 10 minutes, until puffed and light golden.
12. Cool the biscotti on a rack and store in a tin between sheets of wax paper.
Makes 3 dozen.
